gaps: A base-rate prior for SCOTUS certiorari grants (overall and IFP-docket) surfaced directly from the corpus would beat reasoning from memory and reduce variance across runs.
A field on the snapshot flagging IFP vs. paid docket (derivable from the docket number) would make the strongest available signal explicit rather than inferred.
helpful: fedcourts paths to resolve the snapshot/event paths.
schemas/prediction.schema.json as the exact output contract.
Prior claude-baseline predictions on near-identical IFP cert petitions as a calibration anchor.
notes: Snapshot encodes no realized disposition (cert grant/deny/terminated dates all null) and no merits facts, so the call rests on the IFP-docket structural signal and the cert base rate. Did not run fedcourts query: prior runs report its published-opinion dispositions do not map onto cert-stage grant/deny practice.
